description: This group of optical micrographs demonstrates the microstructure evolution of Fe–30Mn–11Al–1.2C low-density steel under different cold rolling reductions, with all subgraphs sharing a 100 μm scale bar, and the axes marked as rolling direction (RD) and normal direction (ND). Subgraph (a) corresponds to 10% cold rolling reduction, (b) to 30%, (c) to 50%, (d) to 70%, and (e) to 90%. For reductions of 10% to 50% (subgraphs a-c), the microstructure shows typical Taylor-lattice-type low energy dislocation structure formed by planar dislocation glide, with gradually refined slip band spacing and inhomogeneous deformation across grains with favorable orientations. For reductions of 70% to 90% (subgraphs d-e), abundant shear bands are observed, grains are significantly elongated along RD, and shear bands increase with reduction to erode almost the entire matrix. The figure supports the conclusion that the microstructure evolution during cold rolling of the steel is divided into two distinct stages.
